rawLog:
Benchmarking crypto.randomUUID (v4) (n=200000, 10 trials)...
  -> 1.520e+6 ± 302,285.6 ops/sec (95% CI 1.304e+6–1.736e+6)
Benchmarking UUIDv7 (custom, RFC 9562) (n=200000, 10 trials)...
  -> 962,768.1 ± 111,920 ops/sec (95% CI 882,710.9–1.043e+6)
Benchmarking SHA-256 hash-derived (v5-style) (n=20000, 10 trials)...
  -> 389,953.7 ± 56,419.2 ops/sec (95% CI 349,596.6–430,310.7)
Benchmarking Math.random (v4-format) (n=200000, 10 trials)...
  -> 848,841.4 ± 26,558 ops/sec (95% CI 829,844.3–867,838.5)
Benchmarking GenoID (proposed, GA-inspired, v8) (n=200000, 10 trials)...
  -> 1.235e+7 ± 799,716.6 ops/sec (95% CI 1.177e+7–1.292e+7)
Benchmarking GenoID-structured (dbkey, v8) (n=200000, 10 trials)...
  -> 618,028.9 ± 12,004.8 ops/sec (95% CI 609,441.7–626,616)
Running collision test, n=2000000 per algorithm...
  crypto.randomUUID (v4): 0 collisions in 2,000,000 samples
  UUIDv7 (custom, RFC 9562): 0 collisions in 2,000,000 samples
  SHA-256 hash-derived (v5-style): 0 collisions in 50,000 samples
  Math.random (v4-format): 0 collisions in 2,000,000 samples
  GenoID (proposed, GA-inspired, v8): 0 collisions in 2,000,000 samples
  GenoID-structured (dbkey, v8): 0 collisions in 2,000,000 samples
Done.
Native baseline: v4=1.520e+6 ops/s, GenoID pool=1.235e+7 ops/s (8.1x faster), structured=618,028.9 ops/s (2.5x slower vs v4).

results.RNG source: Pooled OS CSPRNG (refills every 256 UUIDs) + byte-level GA crossover/mutation
Pooled CSPRNG + declared v8 layout (timestamp/shard/monotonic-counter) + field-boundary crossover + constraint repair
